label: 1722 CE
era_summary: 1722 (MDCCXXII) was a common year starting on Thursday of the Gregorian calendar and a common year starting on Monday of the Julian calendar, the 1722nd year of the Common Era (CE) and Anno Domini (AD) designations, the 722nd year of the 2nd millennium, the 22nd year of the 18th century, and the 3rd year of the 1720s decade. As of the start of 1722, the Gregorian calendar was 11 days ahead of the Julian calendar, which remained in localized use until 1923.

rome_italy.summary: Brecke records no major conflicts touching this region in 1722 (safety scored at the era baseline of 85). No Maddison GDP coverage for this region — economy held at neutral 50, reflecting the thin pre-modern record outside the European core. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).

andalusia_iberia.summary: Active conflicts in 1722: Kakonda-Portugal (Angola), 1716-22; Spain-Texas, 1720-22; Russia-Tatars (Tara, western Siberia), 1722. Maddison records GDP/capita 1674 (PRT, 1722) — used as the economy proxy for the whole region. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).
